analysis_scope.target_subset: Non-memory-corruption issues that could still affect a Rust reimplementation
analysis_scope.excluded: Non-CVE reports are kept as notes but are not treated as CVEs

summary.result: No published CVEs were present in all_cves.json, so there are no known non-memory-corruption CVEs to carry forward into the Rust port.

non_cve_notes.title: How to disable quote processing entirely?
non_cve_notes.port_relevance: The report describes quote-handling behavior that can yield an extremely large output record, which suggests a possible parser logic or resource-exhaustion concern if the Rust port reproduces the same semantics.
non_cve_notes.why_not_listed_in_relevant_cves: It is not a published CVE.

conclusion.statement: Based on the analyzed dataset, there are no published non-memory-corruption CVEs affecting libcsv that need to be preserved as known vulnerabilities in the Rust reimplementation.
